seoTitle: Kensington vs Kenthurst — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kensington and Kenthurst. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kensington and Kenthurst.
heading: Kensington vs Kenthurst — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kensington, New South Wales and Kenthurst, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kenthurst has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Kensington (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kensington or Kenthurst safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kensington has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Kenthurst scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kensington scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Kenthurst scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kensington or Kenthurst?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kenthurst has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
